title: Semiwiki Access Other Apps on Phone
gist: A SemiWiki forum thread reports app-access permission popups appearing across Android phones, Android tablets and Windows when viewing a specific EDA forum post, with a user attributing the behavior to an embedded LinkedIn element rather than the site itself.

insights.claim: Users on Android phones, Android tablets and Windows reported an unexpected 'access other apps' permission popup triggered specifically by viewing a SemiWiki EDA forum post, while the site operator disclaimed enabling any such access.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: A reminder that third-party embeds on content sites can surface intrusive OS-level permission prompts the publisher did not intend, a low-grade trust and security risk for ad/social-embedded media.
insights.quote: Nope. We do not allow that. I'm not even sure where to look.
insights.locator: Daniel Nenni said: I just got similar request

insights.claim: A forum user diagnosed the cross-platform permission popup as likely originating from an embedded LinkedIn element on the page rather than from SemiWiki's own code.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Points to social-media embed scripts as the probable vector for unwanted mobile permission prompts, relevant to publishers weighing embed hygiene.
